goal: Persistent server-side process tracker for dev eval runs. Today `/api/dev/launch-eval` spawns a child and streams output via SSE, but the process state is tied to the request lifetime â€” close the tab or refresh the page and the running eval disappears from the UI (and if the request is aborted, SIGTERM kills the child). Build an in-memory Map<id, {command, startedAt, ringBuffer, exitCode, child}> and expose: (a) POST /api/dev/processes (start; returns id and survives the request), (b) GET /api/dev/processes (list active + recent), (c) GET /api/dev/processes/:id/tail (SSE tail that works after reconnect), (d) DELETE /api/dev/processes/:id (SIGTERM). Refactor /api/dev/launch-eval to delegate through it. ProjectOperator on mount hydrates from GET /api/dev/processes filtered to the current project so refresh preserves visibility. Ring buffer ~2000 lines/process. Dev-only; prod stays 403.
